title: verbascum phoeniceum subsp. stojanovii
intro: Verbascum phoeniceum subsp. stojanovii, also known as the purple mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, such as wounds, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Verbascum phoeniceum subsp. stojanovii include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and congestion, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
